title: Field ID case inconsistency between building-block and whole-packet rules
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/rules.py

Building-block rules (rule IDs 64-66) use PascalCase field IDs, while whole-packet rules (rule IDs 0-6) use lowercase:

COAP_RULE (64): 'CoAP.Version', 'CoAP.Type', 'CoAP.TKL', 'CoAP.Code', 'CoAP.MID'
LINK_LOCAL_COAP_RULE (0): 'CoAP.version', 'CoAP.type', 'CoAP.tkl', 'CoAP.code', 'CoAP.mid'

UDP_PORT_RULE (65): 'UDP.SrcPort', 'UDP.DstPort'
_udp_fields(): 'UDP.src_port', 'UDP.dst_port'

ICMPV6_ECHO_RULE (66): 'ICMPv6.Identifier', 'ICMPv6.Sequence'
_icmpv6_echo_fields(): 'ICMPv6.identifier', 'ICMPv6.sequence'

headers.py parses packets using lowercase field names (matching rules 0-6). If someone attempts to use building-block rules (64-66) with the parsed field dicts, field lookups would fail silently (returning None or KeyError).

Fix: Standardize on one naming convention across all rules, or document that building-block rules use a different convention.

Category: API MISUSE
